잠언 17:1.content: Better is a dry morsel with quietness, than a house full of feasting with strife.

잠언 17:2.content: A servant who deals wisely will rule over a son who causes shame, and shall have a part in the inheritance among the brothers.

잠언 17:3.content: The refining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold, but Yahweh tests the hearts.

잠언 17:4.content: An evildoer heeds wicked lips. A liar gives ear to a mischievous tongue.

잠언 17:5.content: Whoever mocks the poor reproaches his Maker. He who is glad at calamity shall not be unpunished.

잠언 17:6.content: Children’s children are the crown of old men; the glory of children are their parents.

잠언 17:7.content: Arrogant speech isn’t fitting for a fool, much less do lying lips fit a prince.

잠언 17:8.content: A bribe is a precious stone in the eyes of him who gives it; wherever he turns, he prospers.

잠언 17:9.content: He who covers an offense promotes love; but he who repeats a matter separates best friends.

잠언 17:10.content: A rebuke enters deeper into one who has understanding than a hundred lashes into a fool.

잠언 17:11.content: An evil man seeks only rebellion; therefore a cruel messenger shall be sent against him.

잠언 17:12.content: Let a bear robbed of her cubs meet a man, rather than a fool in his folly.

잠언 17:13.content: Whoever rewards evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.

잠언 17:14.content: The beginning of strife is like breaching a dam, therefore stop contention before quarreling breaks out.

잠언 17:15.content: He who justifies the wicked, and he who condemns the righteous, both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.

잠언 17:16.content: Why is there money in the hand of a fool to buy wisdom, since he has no understanding?

잠언 17:17.content: A friend loves at all times; and a brother is born for adversity.

잠언 17:18.content: A man void of understanding strikes hands, and becomes collateral in the presence of his neighbor.

잠언 17:19.content: He who loves disobedience loves strife. One who builds a high gate seeks destruction.

잠언 17:20.content: One who has a perverse heart doesn’t find prosperity, and one who has a deceitful tongue falls into trouble.

잠언 17:21.content: He who becomes the father of a fool grieves. The father of a fool has no joy.

잠언 17:22.content: A cheerful heart makes good medicine, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.

잠언 17:23.content: A wicked man receives a bribe in secret, to pervert the ways of justice.

잠언 17:24.content: Wisdom is before the face of one who has understanding, but the eyes of a fool wander to the ends of the earth.

잠언 17:25.content: A foolish son brings grief to his father, and bitterness to her who bore him.

잠언 17:26.content: Also to punish the righteous is not good, nor to flog officials for their integrity.

잠언 17:27.content: He who spares his words has knowledge. He who is even tempered is a man of understanding.

잠언 17:28.content: Even a fool, when he keeps silent, is counted wise. When he shuts his lips, he is thought to be discerning.
